items.question: %pip vs !pip — why does it matter which you use?
items.answer: %pip installs into the exact environment the kernel runs in, so imports work. !pip runs whatever pip is first on PATH, which may belong to a different Python, leading to 'installed but not importable' errors.

items.question: Line magic (%) vs cell magic (%%)?
items.answer: % applies to the single line it's on (e.g. %timeit expr); %% must be the first line of a cell and applies to the entire cell (e.g. %%time).

items.question: What breaks when you paste magic commands into a .py file?
items.answer: The file errors out, because % / %% magics are IPython features, not valid Python syntax for the standard interpreter.
